title: ExtractionResult
description: Output of a knovas-extract-<lang> implementation for one input document. Every language implementation MUST produce JSON conforming to this schema (cross-validated by golden-corpus tests).

spec_version.description: Semantic version of the ExtractionResult contract this output conforms to.

source.description: Provenance of the input document. Computed by the extractor, not parsed from the file.

mime_type.description: IANA media type detected by header/magic-bytes inspection (NOT by file extension).

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the raw input bytes, lowercase hex.

size_bytes.description: Size of the raw input in bytes.

filename.description: Original filename if available. Never used for MIME detection.

metadata.description: Document-level metadata. All fields nullable; absence means 'unknown', not 'empty'. Field names align with Apache Tika / Dublin Core where applicable.

language.description: BCP-47 language tag if detected (e.g. 'de', 'en-US'). Null if not detectable.

created.description: ISO 8601 timestamp; tolerance ±1 s for cross-language comparison.

extra.description: Format-specific metadata. Keys must be namespaced (e.g. 'pdf:producer', 'docx:revision'). Languages may omit unsupported keys but MUST NOT invent unknown ones.

content.description: Extracted text. `text` is always present; `pages` and `sections` are present only when the format supports them.

text.description: Full extracted text. NFC-normalized, LF newlines, trailing whitespace stripped per line, max one blank line between paragraphs. See docs/tolerances.md.

pages.description: Per-page text (PDF, paginated DOCX). Null when format is not paginated.

sections.description: Heading-derived sections (DOCX, MD, HTML). Null when no headings are present or format is unstructured.

warnings.description: Non-fatal issues encountered during extraction (e.g. 'page 7 had an unrecognized font', 'macros stripped'). Empty array when none.

extractor.description: Identifies the implementation that produced this result. Used by `diff_extraction.py` to attribute regressions.
